task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenClinica dashboard or home screen is visible, indicating a successful login (user 'root').

task_completion.description: Navigate to subject DM-104 within the DM-TRIAL-2024 study
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subject Matrix or View Subject page is open, clearly showing the study 'DM-TRIAL-2024' and the subject identifier 'DM-104'.

task_completion.description: Access the 'Concomitant Medication' repeating event for the subject
task_completion.visual_evidence: The event details or data entry screen for the 'Concomitant Medication' event is open for subject DM-104.

task_completion.description: Record the first occurrence with Start Date 2024-02-15 and Location 'Lisinopril'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A data entry form or saved event record for 'Concomitant Medication' showing the Start Date field as '2024-02-15' and the Location field containing 'Lisinopril'.

task_completion.description: Record the second occurrence with Start Date 2024-02-20 and Location 'Amoxicillin'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A separate data entry form or saved event record for 'Concomitant Medication' showing the Start Date field as '2024-02-20' and the Location field containing 'Amoxicillin'.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenClinica web interface to enter data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenClinica UI, rather than terminal windows executing database commands (e.g., psql).

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenClinica application forms
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is entered into genuine OpenClinica web forms with standard application styling, not typed into a text editor or a mock HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent did not use scripts to bypass manual entry
integrity.visual_evidence: No visible terminal windows running Python, bash, or SQL scripts designed to inject the medication records directly into the backend database.
